summarization.a: Process timing diagram illustrating the standard GaN ALE cycle. Half-cycle A consists of a 5 s gas stabilization followed by a 10 s SF6/Ar plasma (50/150 sccm, 100 mTorr, 100 W ICP) and a 10 s Ar purge. Half-cycle B involves four repeated TMA dose/hold sequences (0.1 s dose, 2 s hold) at 150 mTorr, followed by a 25 s Ar purge.

data_extraction.a:
| Step | Duration | Ar Flow (sccm) | SF₆ Flow (sccm) | Pressure (mTorr) | ICP Power (W) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Gas stabilise | 5 s | 150 | 50 | 100 | 0 |
| Ar/SF₆ Plasma | 10 s | 150 | 50 | 100 | 100 |
| Purge | 10 s | 300 | 0 | - | 0 |
data_extraction.b:
| Step | Duration | Ar Flow (sccm) | TMA Dose | Pressure (mTorr) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Dose | 0.1 s | 10 | On | 150 |
| Hold | 2 s | 10 | Off | 150 |
| Purge | 25 s | 300 | Off | - |

a.question: What is the purpose of the "hold" step after each TMA dose in half-cycle B?
a.answer: The hold step acts as a soak period to increase the exposure time of the TMA precursor to the sample surface. This extended contact time facilitates the ligand-exchange reactions between TMA and the fluorinated GaN surface layer, allowing more complete removal of the modified layer and improving etch efficiency.

a.question: How does the chamber pressure differ between half-cycle A and half-cycle B?
a.answer: Half-cycle A operates at 100 mTorr while half-cycle B operates at 150 mTorr.

a.question: What is the flow rate of SF6 during the plasma step?
Why is a low Ar flow (10 sccm) maintained through the ICP during the TMA dose/hold steps?
a.answer: To limit upstream TMA diffusion into the ICP tube.
